title: Cream puffs
subtitle: Choux pastry balls filled with vanilla Chantilly cream
excerpt: Hollow choux pastry balls baked until crisp and golden, split and filled with vanilla-scented whipped cream. The choux must be completely dry inside before filling or the shells go limp. Makes twenty-four.
sourceNotes: Choux pastry puffs filled with cream (profiteroles and cream puffs) are documented in French patisserie from the eighteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: cream puffs made from a cooked flour-and-butter paste enriched with eggs and filled with whipped cream are documented in the pastry chapter. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: hollow baked paste cases filled with whipped cream are recorded as a confection made from a roux-based paste.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The unfilled shells can be stored in an airtight tin for 24 hours. Fill no more than 1 hour before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 200°C for 18 minutes until golden, then reduce to 170°C for 7 minutes to dry the insides

content.text: Do not open the oven in the first 18 minutes; early cold air causes the puffs to collapse. When they are golden and firm at the end of the bake, pierce each one with a skewer to release trapped steam — this keeps the inside dry. Fill no more than an hour before serving or the cream will begin to seep and the shells will lose their crispness.

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Line two baking trays with paper. Bring the {{whole-milk}}, 125 ml water, {{unsalted-butter}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} to a boil in a medium saucepan. Remove from the heat, add all the {{plain-flour}} at once, and beat vigorously until a smooth ball forms. Return to medium heat and stir for 2 minutes to dry the paste. Remove from the heat and cool for 5 minutes. Beat in the {{eggs}} one at a time until the paste is smooth and glossy and falls in a slow, thick ribbon from the spoon. Pipe or spoon 24 rounds of about 3 cm diameter onto the trays, leaving room for them to spread. Bake for 18 minutes without opening the door, then reduce to 170°C and bake for a further 7 minutes until firm and deeply golden. Pierce each puff with a skewer. Cool completely on a rack.

Whip the {{whipping-cream}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and {{vanilla-extract}} to firm peaks. Fit a piping bag with a star or plain nozzle. Slice the top third off each puff or cut in half. Pipe the cream into the bottom half generously. Replace the lid. Dust with icing sugar just before serving.
